what_it_does: This file is an allocation batch manifest: a JSON array that records six source files from the claude-ds project, each with its relative path and shared absolute root directory, for processing in an interpretive ledger wave.
observations: This is a JSON array of six file records, all sharing project "claude-ds" and root "/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds".
Five of the six entries live under assets/icons; the remaining entry is app/workshop.css.
The file types include CSS, JSX, and JS, suggesting a mix of styling, components, and icon metadata.
There is no symbols array or other metadata inside the file content beyond project, path, and root.
standouts: The batch groups a coherent iconography subsystem (ConceptVIcon.jsx, cv-glyphic.css, cv-glyphics.js, cv-icons.js, cv-meaning.js) with a single workshop CSS file.
All records point to the same absolute local root, making the batch strongly project-local.
conventions: Uses a flat JSON array of uniform objects with project, path, and root fields.
Batch is named by wave/alloc/batch path segment (wave-002/alloc/batch-014.json).
Paths are repository-relative while roots are absolute filesystem paths.
concerns: Absolute root paths may not be portable across environments.
No inclusion rationale or selection criteria is recorded in the file.
The relationship between app/workshop.css and the icons assets is not explained.
notes: A project-local batch manifest that mostly inventories claude-ds icon assets plus one workshop stylesheet.
questions: What criteria placed app/workshop.css in the same batch as the icon assets?
What does wave-002/alloc signify in the broader ledger workflow?
Is this batch awaiting symbol extraction, or was it intentionally left with no symbols?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the path suggests an allocation batch for an interpretive wave, and the content is exactly a list of allocated files with project and root.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to capture a scoped set of claude-ds design substrates—especially icon-related assets and a workshop stylesheet—so they can be tracked, interpreted, or fused as a unit.
novelty: The batch mixes a single app-level CSS file with a tightly related icon glyph/component set, and it carries no extracted symbols, functioning purely as a file allocation record.
contribution: It contributes a project-scoped, root-anchored file manifest that a fused system could retain as a traceable allocation unit, preserving the link between project name, relative path, and source root without imposing interpretation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds project files—mostly icon assets under assets/icons plus app/workshop.css—with their shared absolute root and no extracted symbols.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to register a specific set of design-system source files for an interpretive ledger wave.
